summary: Jason Hoffman from Meridian contacted Aegis support (Sarah Chen) regarding the lack of granular file-level restore capabilities in Aegis Protect. An analyst had accidentally overwritten config files on an EBS-backed EC2 trading platform, requiring a two-hour manual workaround involving spinning up a temporary instance to retrieve the files. Sarah confirmed that file-level restore is not currently supported and documented a formal feature request covering granular file-level restore from EBS snapshots, restore-to-alternate-path functionality, and a file metadata preview capability. The case will be logged, Jason's colleague Marcus Webb will be added, and the CSM will be looped in for follow-up.
actionItems: Sarah Chen: Submit formal feature request (Case 1514) for granular file-level restore from EBS snapshots, alternate path restore, and file metadata preview into the Protect product feedback system
Sarah Chen: Add Marcus Webb to Case 1514 documentation and loop in Meridian's CSM for ongoing updates
Jason Hoffman: Monitor portal for case documentation update expected within the hour
Sarah Chen: Ensure CSM provides Jason with roadmap updates on the feature request to avoid it going untracked

keyMoments.text: Sarah confirms that granular file-level restore is not currently supported in Aegis Protect, validating Jason's suspicion
keyMoments.speaker: Sarah Chen

keyMoments.text: Jason describes the two-hour manual workaround required to restore a handful of config files, highlighting significant operational pain
keyMoments.speaker: Jason Hoffman

keyMoments.text: Jason states his trading platform has a 15-minute RTO for file-level restores, making the current workaround operationally unacceptable
keyMoments.speaker: Jason Hoffman

keyMoments.text: Jason thanks Sarah for being transparent about product limitations rather than making vague promises, expressing appreciation for the honest conversation
keyMoments.speaker: Jason Hoffman
